name: Dexamethasone
executiveSummary: Potent IV/PO corticosteroid for cerebral edema, croup, severe COVID, and nausea prophylaxis.
indications:
## Indications

- Cerebral edema, croup (nebulization/IM according to protocol).
- Chemotherapy-induced and postoperative nausea prophylaxis.
- Fetal lung maturation (obstetric regimen).

## Precautions

- Hyperglycemia, immunosuppression. Taper if used for prolonged periods.

> Adjust according to institutional protocols and medical prescription.

adulto.dose: 4–10 mg IV/IM according to indication; pediatric croup: 0.6 mg/kg single dose.

pediatrico.dose: 0.5 to 2 mg/kg/day in 4 daily doses. Anti-inflammatory: 0.08 to 0.3 mg/kg/day 2 to 4 times daily.
pediatrico.notes: Steroidal anti-inflammatory. May cause edema, hypertension, headache, nausea, vomiting.

neonatal.dose: Fetal lung maturation: obstetric regimen. Avoid prolonged use in newborns.
neonatal.administration: Maternal IM / newborn IV according to protocol.

stability:
## General

- IV stable according to dilution.

## Pediatric chart

- Discard remainder once opened.
adverseEffects:
## Effects

- Hyperglycemia, insomnia, adrenal suppression, infections.

bibliography.citation: American Heart Association. ACLS / PALS / NRP Guidelines.
